active_projects.description: Add f_content and p_content to TemporalContent.lean. These existential extractors (f_content(M)={φ|Fφ∈M}, p_content(M)={φ|Pφ∈M}) complement existing g_content/h_content. Prove basic duality lemmas. Foundation for both Succ relation (discrete track) and DenseTask relation (dense track).

Define Succ(u,v) := g_content(u)⊆v ∧ f_content(u)⊆v∪f_content(v) in new SuccRelation.lean. Prove: Succ implies CanonicalR, g/h duality for Succ pairs, and the single-step forcing theorem (Fφ∈u ∧ FFφ∉u ∧ Succ(u,v) → φ∈v). The single-step forcing theorem is the key result connecting F-nesting depth to witness distance.

Define CanonicalTask(u,n,v) inductively from Succ: base u=v for n=0, step ∃w,Succ(u,w)∧CanonicalTask(w,n,v) for n≥1, converse for n<0. Prove the three TaskFrame axioms (nullity identity, forward compositionality by chain concatenation, converse by definition). Prove bounded witness corollary: F^nφ∈u ∧ F^(n+1)φ∉u implies witness within n Succ-steps.

Prove that under discrete axioms (base+DF+seriality), for any MCS u with F⊤∈u, there exists MCS v with Succ(u,v). Constructs deferral seed g_content(u)∪{φ∨Fφ|Fφ∈u}, proves consistency via DF, extends by Lindenbaum. Symmetric predecessor existence via DB. This is the critical proof obligation that bypasses the covering lemma and replaces discrete_Icc_finite_axiom.

Prove CanonicalR(u,v) ↔ ∃n≥1, CanonicalTask(u,n,v). Forward: CanonicalTask implies CanonicalR by g_content transitivity through Succ-chains via temp_4. Backward: CanonicalR implies some CanonicalTask using successor existence and F-nesting bounds. Also prove existing lemmas (canonical_forward_G, canonical_forward_F) from new definitions as backward-compatibility layer.

Construct a time-indexed FMCS family over Int from Succ-chains: enumerate forward/backward via successor/predecessor existence to build fam:ℤ→MCS satisfying FMCS coherence (forward_G, backward_H, forward_F, backward_P). Instantiate DiscreteCanonicalTaskFrame using CanonicalTask as task_rel. Verify WorldHistory respects_task via Succ-chain propagation.

Wire the Succ-chain FMCS and CanonicalTask-based TaskFrame Int into the unconditional discrete representation theorem: valid_discrete φ → ⊢_discrete φ. Instantiate parametric truth lemma with D=ℤ and the Succ-chain task relation. Once sorry-free, remove discrete_Icc_finite_axiom from DiscreteTimeline.lean and the two unproven axioms from DiscreteSuccSeed.lean.

Define DenseTask(u,q,v) ↔ e(tv)-e(tu)=q using the existing Cantor isomorphism TimelineQuot≃oℚ and the canonicalTaskRel (w+d=w') from DurationTransfer.lean. Verify TaskFrame axioms (trivial from group properties). Prove density interpolation theorem: any positive-duration task has arbitrary rational subdivision. Replaces duration-coarse parametric_canonical_task_rel for the dense case.

Construct a temporally complete BFMCS bundle with families indexed by TimelineQuot satisfying both modal_backward (requires multiple families) and temporal coherence (forward_F, backward_P). Uses timelineQuotFMCS from TimelineQuotCanonical.lean as base family. This is the dense analogue of the discrete Succ-chain FMCS and the key blocker for dense completeness (timelineQuotSingletonBFMCS fails modal_backward).

Wire the TimelineQuot BFMCS and DenseTask-based TaskFrame ℚ into the unconditional dense representation theorem: valid_dense φ → ⊢_dense φ. Instantiate parametric truth lemma with D=TimelineQuot (which carries DenselyOrdered). Use timelineQuot_instantiate_dense to instantiate valid_dense at D=TimelineQuot. Resolves the Task 988 blocker via the DenseTask framework.

Once discrete completeness is proved via Succ-chains (task 15), deprecate the old quotient-based pipeline: DiscreteTimelineElem, DiscreteTimelineQuot, SuccOrder/PredOrder construction, and the orderIsoIntOfLinearSuccPredArch pathway. Mark files as deprecated with doc comments pointing to the new Succ-chain approach. Tasks 989 (discrete algebraic completeness) and 974 (SuccOrder) can be marked [EXPANDED] into tasks 10-15.

Review ParametricCanonical.lean, ParametricTruthLemma.lean, and ParametricRepresentation.lean. Determine whether the parametric infrastructure can be refactored to accept a generic task_rel parameter (not hardcoded duration-coarse relation), enabling both CanonicalTask and DenseTask as instantiations. If feasible, refactor; otherwise document the relationship between parametric (base) and specialized (discrete/dense) paths.

active_projects.description: Replace FlagBFMCS satisfies_at with a canonical TaskFrame using truth_at. The current FlagBFMCS completeness theorem uses an internal satisfies_at relation rather than the official truth_at from the semantic layer. Eliminate satisfies_at entirely and construct a canonical TaskFrame directly from FlagBFMCS data, so that the completeness theorem states: consistent S implies existence of a TaskModel and world where truth_at holds for all φ ∈ S. Key architectural requirements: (1) Define canonical world states from CanonicalMCS, (2) Define the duration domain D parametrically (positions within each Flag chain serve as durations), (3) Define the task relation R on world states using CanonicalR, (4) Construct WorldHistory instances from Flags (each Flag yields a history mapping durations to world states), (5) Define the canonical TaskFrame and TaskModel using these components, (6) Prove the truth lemma directly for truth_at rather than satisfies_at, (7) State and prove the completeness theorem using the canonical valid predicate. The existing FlagBFMCS infrastructure (closedFlags, chainFMCS, modal saturation, temporal completeness) provides all the raw material. The work is restructuring it to fit the official TaskFrame/TaskModel/WorldHistory architecture. This supersedes the validity bridge approach in task 997.

active_projects.description: Prove the derivation of F(phi) -> FF(phi) from the density axiom GGpsi -> Gpsi. Two files have the same sorry with the same mathematical gap: (1) derive_F_to_FF in StagedConstruction/CantorPrereqs.lean (line 111) — needs a DerivationTree for F(phi) -> FF(phi); (2) density_of_canonicalR in Canonical/CanonicalTimeline.lean (line 183) — needs the same derivation to find an intermediate CanonicalR witness. The density axiom is GGpsi -> Gpsi (universal form). The existential dual F(phi) -> FF(phi) is equivalent via contrapositive: F(phi) is ~G(~phi), FF(phi) is ~G(~F(phi)) = ~GG(~phi), so F(phi) -> FF(phi) is ~G(~phi) -> ~GG(~phi), which is the contrapositive of GGpsi -> Gpsi for psi = ~phi. The derivation requires chaining through double-negation and temporal K-distribution. Both sorries are marked TODO (Task 991). Fixing them completes the staged construction pipeline for density proofs and removes the dependency on unproven density helpers.

Redesign the FMP filtration for strict temporal semantics. The 2 sorry theorems in Decidability/FMP/TruthPreservation.lean — mcs_all_future_closure (line 263) and mcs_all_past_closure (line 281) — are deprecated because the temporal T-axiom (Gphi -> phi) is NOT valid under strict semantics. filtration_all_future_forward and filtration_all_past_forward depend on them. The FMP module is separate from the main decidability pipeline (decide is sorry-free), but completing it formally proves the finite model property. Resolution options: (A) restrict FMP statement to serial frames where temporal seriality holds, (B) redesign filtration to avoid temporal reflexivity entirely, (C) prove the filtered model satisfies a weaker correctness property sufficient for the FMP theorem. Note: mcs_finite_model_property in FMP.lean does NOT directly use these sorry lemmas, so the impact is localized to filtration_all_future_forward/backward.

Wire the algebraic base completeness theorem using the FMCS domain transfer lemma (task 995). After task 995 provides the order-embedding CanonicalMCS → Int, fill the 2 sorries in AlgebraicBaseCompleteness.lean (lines 104, 155) to prove valid φ → ⊢ φ for base TM logic. The file already has the right structure (construct_bfmcs_int from CanonicalMCS transfer, then use parametric_algebraic_representation_conditional with D = Int). This supersedes abandoned task 987 and completes the base completeness leg of the three-way completeness suite (base/dense/discrete).

active_projects.description: Add the stability operator (box-dot) to the bimodal formula language. The stability operator quantifies over histories passing through the same world state at a given time: (box-dot)phi at (alpha, t) holds iff phi holds at (beta, t) for all beta in Omega with beta(t) = alpha(t). Requires: (1) extend Formula inductive type with stability constructor, (2) define semantics in TaskModel, (3) add S5(box-dot) axiom schemas (T, 4, B, K), (4) prove box implies box-dot (absorption), (5) prove box-dot commutes with box but NOT with G/H. Per research-002 Section 6: box-dot and G have no valid interaction axioms due to genuine branching.

Implement the Shift-Closed Tense S5 Algebra (STSA) representation theorem. Define STSA as a Lean structure extending BooleanAlgebra with □, G, H, σ operators and interaction axioms. Lift temporal duality σ from swap_temporal to the Lindenbaum quotient. Prove LindenbaumAlg is an STSA instance by wiring existing pieces (BooleanStructure, InteriorOperators, UltrafilterMCS). Restructure ParametricRepresentation into unified STSA representation theorem. Research report 001 provides complete algebraic analysis with ~80% of formalization already existing.

artifacts.summary: Comprehensive STSA design with axiom-to-equation translation, representation theorem architecture, and MF/TF interaction analysis

active_projects.description: Prove dense algebraic completeness using D = Rat. Requires: (1) a sorry-free BFMCS construction over Rat (adapting the Int construction with density-exploiting witness placement), (2) proving the DN axiom is valid in DenseCanonicalTaskFrame Rat (truth lemma shows density axiom holds because Rat is densely ordered), (3) wiring dense_representation_conditional to obtain valid_dense φ → ⊢_dense φ. Does not overlap with task 982 (TimelineQuot approach).
